name: VERACYTE, INC.
business: Veracyte is a global diagnostics company that empowers clinicians with high-value insights to guide and assure patients at pivotal moments in the diagnosis and treatment of cancer. In the United States, the company offers laboratory developed tests (LDTs) for prostate (Decipher Prostate), thyroid (Afirma), breast (Prosigna), and bladder (Decipher Bladder) cancers. Internationally, it provides in vitro diagnostic (IVD) tests, currently focused on the Prosigna Breast Cancer Assay.

exposures.sentence: We are a global diagnostics company that empowers clinicians with the high-value insights they need to guide and assure patients at pivotal moments in the race to diagnose and treat cancer.

catalysts.event: Launch of first MRD test (TrueMRD) for muscle-invasive bladder cancer
catalysts.sentence: We have submitted a tech assessment to MolDx, who administers Medicare reimbursement in our lab jurisdiction and, subject to a positive response, expect to launch our first MRD test in the first half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Launch of Prosigna LDT for the U.S. market
catalysts.sentence: We plan to launch the Prosigna LDT and our first indication of TrueMRD in MIBC in 2026.

catalysts.event: PAMA data reporting period for Afirma GSC, Decipher Prostate, and Decipher Bladder CPT codes
catalysts.sentence: The first PAMA data reporting period for CPT 81546 (Afirma GSC), CPT 81542 (Decipher Prostate), and CPT 0016U (Decipher Bladder) under the current triennial data reporting schedule is expected to occur between May 1 through July 31, 2026 based on the data collection period of January 1 through June 30, 2025.

tamClaims.claim: 334,000 men diagnosed with prostate cancer annually in the U.S.
tamClaims.sentence: An estimated 334,000 men are diagnosed with prostate cancer each year in the United States.

tamClaims.claim: 650,000 people undergo FNA biopsy evaluation for thyroid nodules annually in the U.S.
tamClaims.sentence: Each year in the U.S, approximately 650,000 people undergo fine needle aspiration, or FNA, biopsy evaluation for potentially cancerous thyroid nodules.

tamClaims.claim: 225,000 patients in the U.S. and 270,000 patients in Europe diagnosed with HR+ breast cancer annually eligible for Prosigna
tamClaims.sentence: We estimate that there are approximately 225,000 patients in the United States and 270,000 patients across Europe diagnosed with HR+ disease annually and potentially eligible for the Prosigna Breast Cancer Assay.

tamClaims.claim: 85,000 people diagnosed with bladder cancer annually in the U.S.
tamClaims.sentence: Each year in the United States, approximately 85,000 people are expected to be diagnosed with bladder cancer.

tamClaims.claim: 2.7 million Americans screened annually for lung cancer; 1.6 million lung nodules found incidentally each year
tamClaims.sentence: Approximately 2.7 million Americans are screened annually for lung cancer, and about 1.6 million lung nodules are found incidentally each year.
